articles.context: تواجه السودان أزمة عميقة منذ الإطاحة بالرئيس عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تصاعدت التوترات بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع بقيادة محمد حمدان دقلو (حميدتي). في أبريل 2023، اندلعت اشتباكات عنيفة بين الطرفين، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الوضع الإنساني وتهديد الاستقرار في الخرطوم. هذه الديناميات تعكس فشل المؤسسات العسكرية في إدارة البلاد، مما يثير مخاوف من حرب أهلية جديدة.

articles.title: علماء يكتشفون طريقة لتحويل قشور الفول السوداني لمواد كربونية شبيهة بالجرافين
articles.summary: توصل علماء مختصون إلى استخدام عملي مذهل ومفيد لقشور ومخلفات الفول السوداني التي يتم إتلاف ملايين الأطنان منها سنوياً في مختلف أنحاء العالم، وهو ما يعني أن هذه المخلفات المقرر أن تكون في القمامة ستصبح مفيدة بشكل كبير قريباً. وبحسب التفاصيل التي أوردها تقرير نشره موقع “ساينس أليرت” العلمي المتخصص، واطلعت عليه “العربية.نت”، فقد اكتشف …
articles.context: تعتبر قشور الفول السوداني من المخلفات الزراعية التي تُهدر بكميات كبيرة في السودان، حيث يُنتج البلد نحو 1.5 مليون طن من الفول السوداني سنوياً. في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والبيئية الحالية، يُعد هذا الاكتشاف العلمي خطوة مهمة نحو تعزيز الاستدامة وتقليل النفايات، مما يتماشى مع جهود الحكومة السودانية لتحسين الاقتصاد المحلي وتعزيز الابتكار في مجالات التكنولوجيا الخضراء.

articles.title: طرق صحية لشرب القهوة لتحقيق أقصى استفادة
articles.summary: القهوة من أكثر المشروبات شعبيةً في العالم، حيث يتناولها معظم الناس إما للاستيقاظ صباحًا أو للحفاظ على طاقتهم طوال اليوم، مع ذلك، ووفقًا للخبراء، فإن القهوة أكثر من مجرد مشروب صباحي منعش، فهي مصدر غني بمضادات الأكسدة التي تدعم صحة القلب، وعمليات الأيض، وحتى وظائف الدماغ ولكن السر يكمن في طريقة تحضيرها، في هذا التقرير …
articles.context: تعتبر القهوة جزءًا أساسيًا من الثقافة السودانية، حيث تُقدم في المناسبات الاجتماعية وتُعتبر رمزًا للضيافة. في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والسياسية الحالية، مثل النزاع المستمر في دارفور وتداعياته على الإنتاج الزراعي، يصبح التركيز على طرق صحية لشرب القهوة مهمًا لتعزيز الصحة العامة. كما أن تعزيز استهلاك القهوة يمكن أن يسهم في دعم المزارعين المحليين وتحسين الاقتصاد الوطني.

articles.title: نوبات الصداع النصفى فى الصيف.. 7 طرق بسيطة للوقاية
articles.summary: مع ارتفاع درجات الحرارة في فصل الصيف قد يعانى بعض الناس من نوبات الصداع النصفى وقد يكون بسبب ارتفاع الحرارة والجفاف وعدم انتظام نمط حياتهم، ولكن هذا الصداع لا يظهر فجأة، بل يتراكم تدريجيًا ويمكن الوقاية منه حتى قبل ظهوره، وفقًا لتقرير موقع “Healthsite”. فيما يلى.. 7 طرق ذكية للوقاية من الصداع النصفي قبل حدوثه …
articles.context: في ظل ارتفاع درجات الحرارة في السودان خلال فصل الصيف، يعاني الكثيرون من مشاكل صحية مثل الصداع النصفي، مما يزيد من الضغط على النظام الصحي المتعثر بالفعل بسبب النزاعات المستمرة والأزمة الاقتصادية. منذ اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020، لا تزال التوترات قائمة بين مختلف الفصائل، مما يؤثر على قدرة الناس على الوصول إلى الرعاية الصحية اللازمة.

articles.title: لماذا تصاب بجفاف العين بكثرة فى الصيف؟
articles.summary: مع ارتفاع درجات الحرارة، يركز معظم الناس على الجفاف وضربة الشمس والإرهاق، ولكن هناك مشكلة صيفية أخرى صامتة يتجاهلها الكثيرون، وهي جفاف العينين، حيث يلاحظ الأطباء ارتفاعًا ملحوظًا في حالات جفاف العين خلال موجات الحر، نتيجة للجفاف، واستخدام مكيفات الهواء، والغبار، وقضاء وقت طويل أمام الشاشات، وفقا لموقع تايمز ناو لماذا يزداد جفاف العين في …
articles.context: في ظل ارتفاع درجات الحرارة في السودان، يعاني السكان من تأثيرات الجفاف الشديد، الذي تفاقم بسبب النزاعات المستمرة منذ عام 2019 بين الحكومة والجماعات المسلحة. هذه النزاعات أدت إلى تدهور البنية التحتية الصحية، مما يجعل الوصول إلى الرعاية الطبية لعلاج مشاكل مثل جفاف العين أكثر صعوبة. كما أن استخدام مكيفات الهواء في ظل انقطاع الكهرباء يزيد من تفاقم هذه المشكلة الصحية.

articles.title: نجوى إبراهيم “ماما نجوى” التى رسمت بهجة جيل الثمانينات.. عيد ميلادها
articles.summary: يتزامن اليوم، 28 أبريل، عيد ميلاد الفنانة والإعلامية القديرة نجوى إبراهيم، حصلت نجوى إبراهيم على شهادة الثانوية العامة من مدرسة مصر الجديدة الثانوية بنات، وبدأت مسيرتها كمذيعة في التلفزيون العربي بالقاهرة كانت متزوجة من مروان كنفاني، شقيق الأديب الفلسطيني الشهيد غسان كنفاني. أولت نجوى ابراهيم العمل الإعلامي التلفزيوني اهتمامًا أكبر، فعملت في التلفزيون المصري، وقدمت …
articles.context: يأتي عيد ميلاد نجوى إبراهيم في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واجتماعية كبيرة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. تعكس مسيرتها الإعلامية، التي بدأت في فترة السبعينات والثمانينات، أهمية الإعلام في تشكيل الوعي الثقافي والاجتماعي في ظل الأزمات الحالية، مما يجعل احتفالها بمسيرتها الفنية ذا دلالة خاصة في سياق الأحداث الراهنة.

articles.title: UN condemns drone attack on UNHCR aid truck in Darfur
articles.summary: The spokesperson for UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Monday joined the UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR) The post UN condemns drone attack on UNHCR aid truck in Darfur appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The drone attack on the UNHCR aid truck in Darfur comes amid escalating violence following the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, primarily between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). This conflict has led to a humanitarian crisis, displacing millions and severely hampering aid efforts. The UN's condemnation highlights the increasing risks faced by humanitarian workers in a region already fraught with instability since the Darfur conflict began in 2003.

articles.title: UNMISS responds to Tonj violence, conducts patrol in Jebel Iraq
articles.summary: The United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) over the weekend responded to intercommunal violence, The post UNMISS responds to Tonj violence, conducts patrol in Jebel Iraq appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The intercommunal violence in Tonj, South Sudan, is rooted in longstanding ethnic tensions, particularly between the Dinka and Nuer communities. This violence escalated following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the country but has struggled to address local grievances. UNMISS's patrols in Jebel Iraq are part of their ongoing efforts to protect civilians and maintain peace amid a fragile security situation exacerbated by economic hardships and political instability.

articles.title: جيمس سيفشوك : مايكل جاكسون كان أسوأ من جيفرى إبستين
articles.summary: أصدر جيمس سيفشوك بيانًا جديدًا عبر فيه عن دعمه لضحايا الاعتداء الجنسي على الأطفال، وذلك بالتزامن مع عرض فيلم السيرة الذاتية Michael في دور السينما، مع وصفه لـ ملك البوب الراحل مايكل جاكشون أنه أسوأ من جيفري إبستين. ويعد سيفشوك أحد الشخصيات الرئيسية في الفيلم الوثائقي Leaving Neverland، الذي أخرجه دان ريد وعرض عبر شبكة …
articles.context: تأتي تصريحات جيمس سيفشوك في وقت حساس حيث يواجه العالم نقاشات متزايدة حول الاعتداء الجنسي على الأطفال، خاصة بعد عرض فيلم "Michael" الذي يعيد تسليط الضوء على حياة مايكل جاكسون. في السودان، حيث تعاني البلاد من أزمات سياسية واجتماعية، تبرز هذه القضية كجزء من حوار أوسع حول حقوق الضحايا والمساءلة، مما يجعلها ذات أهمية خاصة في سياق التغيرات الاجتماعية الجارية.

articles.title: Opinion| Sovereignty as strategy: China’s role in South Sudan’s stalled peace process

articles.context: South Sudan has been embroiled in a civil war since 2013, following a power struggle between President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace but has faced significant implementation challenges. China's recent advocacy for South Sudan's sovereignty at the UN Security Council highlights its strategic interests in the region amid ongoing tensions and stalled peace efforts.

articles.context: نور الشريف، المعروف كأحد أبرز الفنانين المصريين، بدأ حياته الرياضية في نادي الزمالك خلال فترة الستينيات، وهي فترة شهدت فيها مصر تغييرات اجتماعية وثقافية كبيرة. في ظل الأزمات السياسية والاقتصادية التي تعاني منها المنطقة حاليًا، يعكس ارتباط الشريف بكرة القدم والرياضة بشكل عام أهمية الثقافة الرياضية في تعزيز الهوية الوطنية، خاصة في ظل التوترات المستمرة بين الدول في منطقة القرن الإفريقي.

articles.title: ياسمين صبرى بين فيلمين دفعة واحدة من كريم عبد العزيز لمعتصم النهار
articles.summary: تنشغل الفنانة ياسمين صبري خلال الفترة الحالية بمجموعة من الأعمال الهامة وذلك تعويضاً عن غيابها عن الدراما مؤخراً اذ تعكف على تصوير فيلمين دفعة واحدة . الفيلم الاول يجمعها مع كريم عبد العزيز ويعد التعاون الثاني لهما ويحمل اسم مطلوب عائليًا ، فى حين الفيلم الثاني هو نصيب والذي يجمعها مع معتصم النهار . ويعدا …
articles.context: تأتي عودة ياسمين صبري إلى الساحة الفنية في وقت حساس للدراما المصرية، حيث تشهد صناعة السينما تحديات كبيرة بسبب تأثيرات جائحة كورونا والأزمات الاقتصادية. كما أن التعاون بين صبري وكريم عبد العزيز، أحد أبرز نجوم السينما، يعكس محاولات استعادة الزخم الفني في ظل المنافسة المتزايدة من منصات البث الرقمي. هذا التوجه يأتي بعد غيابها عن الأعمال الدرامية منذ عام 2021، مما يزيد من أهمية مشاريعها الحالية.

articles.title: شرطة البحر الأحمر تكشف تفاصيل اقتحام قسم ديم موسى في بورتسودان
articles.summary: أعلنت شرطة ولاية البحر الأحمر، الاثنين، تفاصيل حادثة دخول مجموعة من المواطنين إلى قسم شرطة ديم موسى في بورتسودان، بعد توقيف مشتبه به في جريمة سرقة. وقالت الشرطة في بيان إن عدداً من المواطنين العاملين في تأمين الأحياء ألقوا القبض على المتهم وأحضروه إلى القسم لاتخاذ الإجراءات القانونية، مشيرة إلى أنهم حاولوا دخول القسم جماعياً …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الحادثة في ظل تصاعد التوترات الأمنية في بورتسودان، حيث تشهد المدينة صراعات بين الجماعات المسلحة والسلطات المحلية منذ اندلاع النزاع في السودان في أبريل 2023. كما أن الوضع السياسي غير المستقر، الناتج عن الصراع بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، يزيد من تعقيد جهود الحفاظ على الأمن في المناطق الحضرية، مما يجعل تدخل المواطنين في قضايا الأمن المحلي أكثر شيوعاً.

articles.title: استعادة تشغيل المناجم في المثلث الحدودي بين السودان وليبيا ومصر
articles.summary: أكدت مصادر من مناطق التعدين الأهلي في المثلث الحدودي بين السودان وليبيا ومصر، الاثنين، استئناف نشاط التعدين تحت إشراف قوات الدعم السريع بعد توقف استمر عدة أشهر. وقالت المصادر وفق منصة دارفور 24 إن العمل عاد في المناجم المحيطة بسوق المثلث، خصوصاً المواقع القريبة من الحدود الليبية التشادية قرب بوابة “الدبابة”. وذكر أحد المعدنين أن …
articles.context: تأتي استعادة تشغيل المناجم في المثلث الحدودي بين السودان وليبيا ومصر في وقت حساس، حيث تشهد السودان صراعات داخلية متزايدة منذ اندلاع النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. هذا النزاع أثر بشكل كبير على الاقتصاد المحلي، مما يجعل استئناف التعدين تحت إشراف قوات الدعم السريع خطوة مهمة لتعزيز الموارد المالية في ظل الأوضاع المتدهورة.

articles.title: South Sudan pitches minerals to U.S.
articles.summary: South Sudan is ready to offer mining contracts to the United States, Finance Minister Salvatore The post South Sudan pitches minerals to U.S. appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan, which gained independence from Sudan in 2011, has faced ongoing internal conflict and economic challenges, particularly following the civil war from 2013 to 2018. By courting U.S. mining interests, South Sudan hopes to leverage its untapped mineral resources to foster economic growth and strengthen ties with Washington amid regional instability.

articles.title: شاهد بالفيديو.. الفنان سجاد بحري يفاجئ الجميع ويعلن خطوبته من الفتاة الأمريكية: (خطيبتي وحتبقى زوجتي.. أبوسها وأعمل معاها العاوز أعمله)

articles.context: سجاد بحري، فنان سوداني معروف، يأتي في وقت تشهد فيه السودان تحولات سياسية واجتماعية كبيرة بعد الثورة التي أطاحت بنظام عمر البشير في 2019. بينما يسعى الشباب السوداني للتعبير عن هويتهم وثقافتهم، تثير مثل هذه الأحداث الجدل حول التقاليد والعلاقات بين الثقافات. كما أن الانفتاح على العالم الخارجي، خاصة مع العلاقات المتنامية مع الولايات المتحدة، يجعل من هذه القصة ذات دلالة في سياق التغييرات الاجتماعية.

articles.title: شاهد.. “بقال” ينشر فيديو له أيام قيادته لجنود المليشيا بالخرطوم: (سيشهد التاريخ أن “الامكعوكات” عيال “الماهرية” كانوا يحرسوني ويفتحوا لي أبواب العربية)
articles.summary: نشر الصحفي, والقيادي السابق بمليشيا الدعم السريع, إبراهيم بقال سراج, مقطع فيديو ساخر وذلك على حسابه عبر موقع التواصل الاجتماعي فيسبوك. وبحسب رصد ومتابعة محرر موقع النيلين, فقد استذكر “بقال”, عبر المقطع أيام عمله وقيادته لجنود مليشيا الدعم السريع, عندما تم تعيينه وقتها والياً لولاية الخرطوم. وكتب القيادي السابق بالمليشيا ساخراً من الهجوم الذي ظل …
articles.context: إبراهيم بقال سراج هو قيادي سابق في مليشيا الدعم السريع، التي لعبت دورًا محوريًا في النزاع المستمر في السودان منذ اندلاع الاحتجاجات ضد نظام عمر البشير في 2019. بعد الإطاحة بالبشير، تم تعيينه والياً للخرطوم، مما زاد من تعقيد الصراع بين القوات المسلحة والمليشيات. يأتي نشر الفيديو في وقت حساس حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين المليشيات والقوات الحكومية عقب الانقلاب العسكري في أكتوبر 2021.

articles.title: شاهد الفيديو الذي خطف الأضواء ونال إعجاب المتابعين.. طلاب سودانيون يحملون أستاذهم على طريقة أصحاب البطولات ويحتفلون معه بشكل ملفت
articles.summary: تصدر مقطع فيديو متداول على مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي, “الترند”, بعد انتشاره على نطاق واسع عبر السوشيال ميديا بالسودان. وبحسب ما شاهد محرر موقع النيلين, فإن الفيديو ظهر فيه طلاب سودانيون, يحتفلون بأساتذهم “المحبوب”, بطريقة ملفتة نالت إعجاب المتابعين. الطلاب, حملوا معلمهم “شيخ هاشم”, واحتفلوا به داخل حرم المدرسة, على طريقة صناع البطولات, وسط إعجاب المتابعين …
articles.context: في ظل الأوضاع السياسية المتقلبة في السودان، حيث يعاني البلد من صراعات داخلية منذ الإطاحة بالرئيس عمر البشير في 2019، يبرز هذا الفيديو كرمز للأمل والوحدة بين الشباب. يعكس الاحتفال بمدرسهم "شيخ هاشم" رغبة الطلاب في تعزيز الروابط الاجتماعية الإيجابية في وقت تتزايد فيه التحديات الاقتصادية والأمنية. كما يعكس هذا الحدث أهمية التعليم ودور المعلمين في تشكيل مستقبل البلاد.

articles.title: Court gives DPP last chance in Starlet Wahu murder case
articles.summary: DPP ordered to avail key witness from Mbagathi Level Five.
articles.context: The murder case of Starlet Wahu has drawn significant public attention in Kenya, particularly due to the involvement of high-profile figures and the broader implications for justice in cases involving women. The case highlights ongoing concerns about gender-based violence and the efficacy of the Kenyan judicial system, especially following the 2017 "Not Yet Uhuru" protests that called for accountability and reform in handling such cases. The DPP's struggle to present key witnesses underscores these systemic challenges.

articles.title: Impeachment trial: Gachagua's day in court
articles.summary: Afternon session resumes as Gachagua challenges his removal from office.
articles.context: The impeachment trial of Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua comes amid heightened political tensions following the August 2022 elections, which saw President William Ruto's administration face criticism over governance issues. Gachagua's removal is being challenged in a context where the opposition, led by Raila Odinga, has been vocal against perceived government overreach and corruption. This trial could significantly impact the fragile political landscape and Ruto's administration's stability as it navigates ongoing economic challenges.

articles.title: شاهد.. آخر فيديو للمثل الراحل مختار بخيت “الدعيتر”.. ظهر وهو يتبادل القفشات والضحكات مع أصدقائها قبل ساعات من وفاته
articles.summary: تداول رواد مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي بالسودان, مقطع فيديو مؤثر للممثل, والدرامي, السوداني, الراحل مختار بخيت, الشهير بالدعيتر. وكان الممثل, المحبوب قد رحل الأسبوع الماضي, بعد تعرضه لأزمة صحية دخل على إثرها إحدى المسشفيات بالمملكة العربية السعودية, ليرحل بعدها مخلفاً حزناً بالغاً عند أصدقائها ومتابعيه. وبحسب رصد ومتابعة محرر موقع النيلين, فإن الفيديو الذي ظهر فيه …
articles.context: مختار بخيت، المعروف بالدعيتر، كان رمزاً ثقافياً في السودان، حيث ساهم في تعزيز الفنون الدرامية في البلاد. رحيله المفاجئ يأتي في وقت تعاني فيه السودان من أزمات سياسية واقتصادية متزايدة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في عام 2023 بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع، مما زاد من الحاجة إلى الفنون كوسيلة للتعبير عن المعاناة والأمل في ظل الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: صحفي سعودي بارز يكشف لأول مرة عن دعم مصري عسكري للخليج
articles.summary: علق الصحفي السعودي الشهير عبد الرحمن الراشد، على الجدل الدائر في الأوساط الخليجية بشأن الموقف المصري من حرب إيران والهجمات على دول الخليج، مؤكدا أنْ لا مصلحة في “خسارة مصر”.وقال الراشد في بودكاست “مزيج”، إن مصر دولة كبيرة ومركزية ومهمة للغاية من الناحية الجيوسياسية، وأن الخليج سيجدها إلى جانبه كلما تعقدت الأزمة التي لا يعرف …
articles.context: تأتي تصريحات عبد الرحمن الراشد في وقت حساس، حيث تتصاعد التوترات في المنطقة بسبب تصاعد الأنشطة الإيرانية في الخليج، خاصة بعد الهجمات على المنشآت النفطية في السعودية والإمارات عام 2019. كما أن العلاقات بين مصر ودول الخليج، خصوصاً السعودية والإمارات، شهدت تقارباً ملحوظاً بعد دعم مصر لجهود التحالف العربي في اليمن منذ عام 2015، مما يعكس أهمية التعاون الإقليمي في مواجهة التحديات الأمنية.

articles.title: Witness: Cameras were functional before Endarasha fire tragedy
articles.summary: CCTV expert says all 11 cameras at the school were functional days before deadly fire.
articles.context: The Endarasha fire tragedy occurred on January 24, 2023, at a school in Nyeri County, Kenya, resulting in the deaths of several students. This incident has reignited discussions on school safety and accountability, particularly in light of the ongoing scrutiny of the Kenyan government's commitment to improving infrastructure in educational institutions following the 2018 National Education Sector Plan. The functioning of CCTV cameras is crucial for transparency in investigations into such tragedies.

articles.title: Restitution, Global Governance on Agenda at Inaugural Africa-France Summit in Nairobi
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Nairobi, Kenya, to attend France’s first summit with English-speaking African countries, scheduled for 11–12 May 2026 under the theme Africa Forward. At a joint briefing held today at the French Embassy in Addis Ababa, the ambassadors of France and Kenyan Ambassador outlined the summit’s agenda. French Ambassador Alexis […]
articles.context: The inaugural Africa-France Summit comes at a pivotal moment as France seeks to strengthen ties with English-speaking African nations amid shifting geopolitical dynamics. Following the 2020 France-Africa Summit, which faced criticism for its focus on Francophone countries, this new initiative aims to address historical grievances and promote equitable partnerships. Additionally, ongoing tensions in the Horn of Africa, particularly the conflict in Ethiopia and regional instability, underscore the urgency for collaborative governance and conflict resolution strategies.

articles.title: Two Kenyans among 14 killed in South Sudan plane crash
articles.summary: All 13 passengers and pilot killed after aircraft crashes on the outskirts of Juba.
articles.context: The plane crash near Juba highlights ongoing security challenges in South Sudan, a country still grappling with the aftermath of a civil war that erupted in 2013. The conflict has led to a fragile peace, with the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) signed in September 2018 struggling to maintain stability. The presence of foreign nationals, including Kenyans, underscores the region's interconnectedness amid ongoing humanitarian and security crises.

articles.title: Suspects in Scot's murder in Kenya charged over attack on another man
articles.summary: The four men pleaded not guilty to targeting an American in an alleged attack at a holiday apartment.
articles.context: The murder of the Scottish national in Kenya has heightened concerns about security for expatriates and tourists, particularly in popular regions like Nairobi and the coastal areas. This incident follows a series of violent crimes against foreigners, prompting the Kenyan government to enhance security measures. The ongoing challenges of crime and tourism safety are critical as Kenya seeks to recover from the economic impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and restore international confidence.

articles.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills all 15 on board
articles.summary: The authorities are investigating but early reports suggest bad weather and poor visibility may be to blame.
articles.context: The plane crash in South Sudan occurs amidst ongoing instability following the civil war that lasted from 2013 to 2018, which left the country deeply divided. The peace agreement signed in September 2018 has seen fragile progress, but tensions remain high, particularly with the recent clashes in Unity State. This incident raises concerns about the safety of air travel in a region where infrastructure is still recovering from years of conflict and neglect.

articles.title: Ethiopia Pushes for Climate Finance as CIF Launches New Resilience Program
articles.summary: Ethiopia is ramping up efforts to secure climate finance and advance green development as global partners unveil a new resilience investment program at the opening of the Climate Investment Funds (CIF) Global Knowledge Exchange, which runs from April 27 to 30, 2026 in Addis Ababa. Opening the event, Ahmed Shide, minister of Finance, said climate […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant climate challenges, exacerbated by ongoing conflicts, particularly the Tigray War that began in November 2020, which has hindered development and resource allocation. The country is also a signatory to the Paris Agreement and has committed to ambitious climate goals, making the CIF's new resilience program crucial for securing funding to address both climate adaptation and recovery efforts. The event in Addis Ababa marks a pivotal moment for Ethiopia's green development strategy amidst these pressing issues.

articles.title: Tribunal blocks FKF leadership shake-up, reinstates Hussein Mohammed
articles.summary: FKF boss back in office after Sports Disputes Tribunal blocks his ouster.
articles.context: The Football Kenya Federation (FKF) has faced ongoing turmoil since the government disbanded its leadership in November 2021, citing mismanagement and corruption. Hussein Mohammed, who was reinstated by the Sports Disputes Tribunal, had previously been ousted amid a power struggle that involved rival factions within the FKF and the Ministry of Sports. This ruling comes as Kenya seeks to stabilize its football governance ahead of upcoming international competitions and FIFA's scrutiny of its football administration.

articles.title: Kiir briefed on investment prospects after IMF–World Bank meetings
articles.summary: President Salva Kiir has been briefed on potential international support for economic recovery and investment opportunities following South Sudan’s participation in the IMF–World Bank Spring Meetings. The update was delivered by a government delegation led by Finance Minister Salvatore Garang Mabiordit after attending the 2026 Spring Meetings in Washington, D.C. Officials said discussions with the […] The post Kiir briefed on investment prospects after IMF–World Bank meetings first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing economic challenges and conflict since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by civil war from 2013 to 2018. The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, signed in 2018, aimed to stabilize the country but implementation has been slow. The recent IMF–World Bank meetings in April 2026 are crucial as they present an opportunity for South Sudan to secure international investment and support for its fragile economy.

articles.summary: South Sudan’s justice minister on Monday dismissed as fabricated a document circulating on social media The post Makuei rejects claims linking Kiir family to graft appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The allegations against President Salva Kiir's family come amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan, where corruption has been a persistent issue since the country’s independence in 2011. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace but has struggled to curb corruption and improve governance. The recent claims could further destabilize the fragile political landscape as the country approaches the 2024 elections.

articles.title: Kindiki's mountain incursion in do-or-die fight with Gachagua
articles.summary: Kindiki-Gachagua rivalry threatens to split Mt Kenya vote.
articles.context: The rivalry between Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua and Interior Cabinet Secretary Kithure Kindiki has intensified following the 2022 general elections, where Gachagua emerged as a key figure in President William Ruto's administration. With the Mt. Kenya region historically pivotal in Kenyan politics, their contest for influence threatens to fracture the region's voting bloc ahead of the 2027 elections, particularly as both leaders seek to consolidate support among local constituents and assert their political dominance.

articles.context: The ongoing political tensions in Ethiopia, particularly following the Tigray conflict that escalated in November 2020, have led to increased demand for upscale housing in Addis Ababa as expatriates and affluent locals seek stability. The signing of the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022 has somewhat eased tensions, but the economic recovery remains fragile, making prime real estate like the penthouse in Bole increasingly attractive for those looking for both luxury and security in a volatile environment.

articles.title: Invitation For Bid
articles.summary: The International Rescue Committee (IRC) is one of the world’s leading humanitarian and development organizations, operating in over 40 countries to deliver relief, rehabilitation, and post-crisis recovery services. Since initiating operations in Ethiopia in 2000 in response to severe drought, the IRC has implemented integrated emergency and development programs across six regions of Ethiopia. Its programming spans […]
articles.context: The International Rescue Committee (IRC) has been operating in Ethiopia since 2000, responding to crises such as the 2015 El Niño-induced drought and the ongoing Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The humanitarian situation has worsened due to intercommunal violence and displacement, exacerbating food insecurity and health challenges. As Ethiopia navigates a fragile peace process following the November 2022 ceasefire agreement, the IRC's role in providing essential services remains critical.

articles.context: The mobile court in Leer County is part of a broader initiative by the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) to enhance access to justice in a region still grappling with the aftermath of the civil war that erupted in December 2013. This conflict has left deep social and legal voids, particularly in Unity State, where local governance and judicial systems remain severely weakened. The court's proceedings are crucial for addressing impunity and restoring trust in legal institutions.

The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, signed in September 2018, aimed to end hostilities, but violence and insecurity persist, impacting air travel safety and operations. This incident raises concerns about the reliability of aviation services in a nation still recovering from years of conflict.

articles.title: Civil Society Coalition launches $50,000 fundraiser for Greater Mundri relief
articles.summary: A coalition of 23 civil society organizations has launched a grassroots fundraising campaign to provide urgent peacebuilding and humanitarian assistance to conflict-affected communities in Greater Mundri, Western Equatoria State. articles.context: Greater Mundri has faced escalating violence and instability since the outbreak of civil war in South Sudan in 2013, exacerbated by ethnic tensions and competition for resources. The recent clashes between government forces and armed groups, particularly in early 2023, have displaced thousands and hindered humanitarian access. The ongoing peace negotiations, including the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, have yet to fully stabilize the region, highlighting the urgent need for grassroots support.

articles.title: Blankets & Wine ordered to pay Sh300,000 for unauthorised use of woman’s image
articles.summary: Complaint argued that the use of her likeness falsely implied endorsement.
articles.context: The ruling against Blankets & Wine comes amid heightened scrutiny of intellectual property rights in Kenya, particularly following the 2021 enactment of the Copyright Amendment Bill, which aims to strengthen protections for creators. This case highlights ongoing tensions between businesses and individuals over image rights, reflecting broader concerns about consumer trust and brand integrity in a rapidly evolving digital landscape. The decision underscores the need for companies to navigate these rights carefully as they engage with local communities.

articles.title: Renewed violence in Tonj East leaves 47 dead, dozens injured
articles.summary: Two separate incidents of violence in Tonj East Countythat have left at least 47 people dead and dozens injured, according to authorities in Warrap State. articles.context: The violence in Tonj East is rooted in longstanding ethnic tensions between the Luachjang and Anei-Atak communities, exacerbated by the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to stabilize the region but has struggled to address local grievances. The recent clashes reflect ongoing struggles for resources and power, highlighting the fragile peace in Warrap State amid a backdrop of unresolved disputes and historical rivalries.

articles.title: Justice ministry dismisses alleged “Bol Mel leak report” as fabricated
articles.summary: The Ministry of Justice and Constitutional Affairs has dismissed as fabricated a document circulating on digital platforms alleging a leaked investigation report involving former Vice President Benjamin Bol Mel. Former Vice President Benjamin Bol Mel has been a controversial figure, and any allegations against him could exacerbate existing rivalries within the ruling Sudan People's Liberation Movement (SPLM) and impact the fragile peace process.

articles.title: Two brothers among those killed in Kitui attack
articles.summary: Seven people killed after masked assailants storm Kwa Kamari Township.
articles.context: The Kitui attack occurs against a backdrop of rising insecurity in Kenya, particularly in regions bordering the volatile eastern provinces. The resurgence of banditry and intercommunal violence has intensified since the 2019 peace agreement between the Pokot and Turkana communities, which aimed to curb cattle rustling. This incident highlights ongoing challenges in maintaining security and stability in rural areas, exacerbated by political tensions ahead of the 2027 general elections.

articles.title: Sabastian Sawe's marathon record boosts Adidas in 'supershoe' race with Nike
articles.summary: German sportswear maker Adidas' ADSGn.DE shares rose on Monday after Kenya's Sabastian Sawe...
articles.context: Sabastian Sawe's recent marathon record of 2:00:29, set at the 2023 Valencia Marathon, highlights Kenya's dominance in long-distance running and its athletes' growing influence on global sportswear markets. This achievement comes amid increasing competition between Adidas and Nike, particularly after Nike's controversial marketing strategies and athlete endorsements. The rivalry intensified following the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, where both brands sought to capitalize on the success of elite Kenyan runners.

articles.title: Family’s plea on Kenyan detained by ICE in US
articles.summary: Kenyan family trying to raise Sh2 million to secure release of their kin in the US.
articles.context: The plight of the Kenyan family seeking to raise Sh2 million for their detained relative in the U.S. highlights the ongoing challenges faced by East African migrants amid stricter immigration policies under the Trump administration, which began in 2017. This situation is exacerbated by Kenya's own struggles with corruption and human rights issues, prompting many to seek better opportunities abroad, often leading to precarious legal situations in host countries like the U.S.

articles.title: Last contingent of Kenyan police in Haiti set to return
articles.summary: Return of final 150 officers concludes Kenya's operation in Haiti.
articles.context: Kenya's deployment of police to Haiti began in 2021 as part of a United Nations mission to stabilize the country following the assassination of President Jovenel Moïse in July 2021. The return of the final 150 officers marks the end of a significant international effort to address Haiti's escalating gang violence and political instability, which has persisted despite various peace agreements and interventions, including the 2022 agreement for a multinational security support mission.

The aviation sector has struggled with safety issues, exacerbated by the lack of infrastructure and regulatory oversight. This incident highlights the risks faced by civilians traveling within a nation still grappling with the aftermath of war and economic challenges, particularly in remote areas like Luri.

articles.title: Court told of how man sold 'Nation' ePapers on WhatsApp
articles.summary: Mr Koskei, NMG's head of security, was the first witness to take the witness stand.
articles.context: The case comes amid ongoing concerns over intellectual property rights and digital piracy in Kenya, particularly following the 2021 Copyright Amendment Act aimed at strengthening protections for content creators. The incident highlights the challenges faced by media companies like Nation Media Group (NMG) in the digital age, where unauthorized distribution of content via platforms like WhatsApp undermines their revenue streams and raises questions about enforcement of copyright laws in a rapidly evolving media landscape.

articles.title: UK pursues fraudster in Sh219m scandal believed to be in Kenya
articles.summary: British authorities believed he is hiding in Nairobi's Spring Valley following the scam.

This case underscores ongoing concerns about corruption and financial integrity in Kenya, particularly in light of the country’s recent efforts to combat graft ahead of the 2024 elections.

articles.title: How special Sawe broke iconic sub-two-hour barrier
articles.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe on becoming the first person to break the two-hour barrier for the marathon.
articles.context: Kenya has a storied history in long-distance running, producing legends like Eliud Kipchoge, who famously broke the two-hour marathon barrier in a controlled setting in 2019. The recent success of Sabastian Sawe comes amid a resurgence of interest in athletics, particularly following the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, where Kenyan athletes excelled. This achievement not only highlights Kenya's dominance in the sport but also serves as a source of national pride during a time of economic challenges and political transitions.

articles.title: President Kiir urged to withdraw peace agreement amendment bill

articles.context: The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement (R-ARCSS) aimed to end South Sudan's prolonged civil war, which erupted in 2013. Recent tensions have emerged as President Salva Kiir's government seeks to amend the agreement without broad consensus, raising concerns among civil society leaders like Edmond Yakani. The proposed amendments threaten to undermine the fragile peace and the collaborative governance structures established to foster stability in the country.

articles.title: Court rejects live broadcast and adjournment in Machar trial; Questioning set for May 4

articles.context: تتزامن هذه المعلومات مع تصاعد النزاع في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين قوات الدعم السريع والجيش السوداني، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. كما أن الدعم الخارجي، بما في ذلك التمويل الإماراتي، يعكس دور القوى الإقليمية في الصراع، حيث تسعى الإمارات لتعزيز نفوذها في المنطقة. التحقيق يكشف عن استخدام المرتزقة الكولومبيين كجزء من استراتيجية عسكرية معقدة تدعمها قوى خارجية.

articles.title: 'I cried so hard' - the Kenyan WNBA star who beat US visa heartbreak
articles.summary: Madina Okot only started playing basketball six years ago, but the Kenyan's rapid rise has seen her set a WNBA milestone at the age of 21.
articles.context: Madina Okot's journey comes at a time when Kenya is grappling with a youth unemployment crisis, with over 70% of its population under 30. The Kenyan government has been promoting sports as a viable career path, especially following the 2021 Sports Act aimed at enhancing athlete welfare. Okot's success in the WNBA not only highlights individual achievement but also serves as an inspiration amidst the country's ongoing struggles for economic opportunities and representation in global sports.

articles.title: Wildberries marks official launch of its e-commerce marketplace in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Wildberries, the international e-commerce giant, has entered Ethiopia by opening its platform to local sellers. Its broader strategy focuses on adapting its global model to the local market. It has confirmed it is working closely with local partners to enable smoother last-mile delivery and payment integration, including enabling transactions in Birr. The company noted that […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's e-commerce landscape has been evolving amid ongoing political and economic challenges, particularly following the Tigray conflict that escalated in late 2020. The government's push for digital transformation and economic recovery has created a favorable environment for foreign investment, particularly in technology and e-commerce. Wildberries' entry aligns with the Ethiopian government's 2021 Digital Ethiopia 2025 strategy, which aims to enhance digital infrastructure and promote local entrepreneurship.

articles.title: UAE-backed Colombian mercenaries provided support to Sudan paramilitary, report says
articles.summary: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, particularly the power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), has escalated since April 2023. The UAE's involvement, particularly through the recruitment of Colombian mercenaries, highlights its strategic interest in the region, especially following the 2020 normalization agreement with Israel. This support for the RSF raises concerns about the UAE's role in exacerbating instability in Sudan and the broader Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Firms up Order for Six Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner Jets
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines has converted the option to buy six Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner jets into a firm order, according to the US plane maker. Ethiopian and Boeing announced the
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa's largest airline, has been pivotal in boosting the country's economy and connectivity since its establishment in 1945. Amid ongoing political tensions and conflict in the Tigray region, the airline's expansion reflects a commitment to recovery and growth. The firm order for the Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners, announced in October 2023, underscores Ethiopia's strategic focus on enhancing its aviation sector and re-establishing its status as a regional hub following the 2020-2022 Tigray conflict.

articles.title: Somali intelligence says over 33 al-Shabaab terrorists killed in Middle Shabelle operation
articles.summary: Coordinated raids disrupted planned attacks by group gathering around 90 fighters, NISA adds
articles.context: The recent operation in Middle Shabelle comes amid heightened tensions following the Somali government's intensified military campaign against al-Shabaab, particularly since President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's re-election in May 2022. The group's resurgence has been marked by increased attacks, prompting the National Intelligence and Security Agency (NISA) to ramp up counter-terrorism efforts. This operation is significant as it disrupts al-Shabaab's attempts to consolidate power and recruit fighters ahead of the upcoming electoral period in Somalia.

articles.title: Nearly 4M displaced returned across Sudan amid fragile conditions: UN migration agency
articles.summary: IOM says lack of housing, health care, jobs could make returns unsustainable
articles.context: Sudan has faced prolonged instability since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019, leading to ongoing conflict between military factions and various armed groups. The 2021 coup exacerbated humanitarian crises, displacing millions. The recent clashes in Darfur and the ongoing power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces have intensified the challenges for returnees, making the International Organization for Migration's warnings about unsustainable returns particularly urgent.

articles.title: Crisis in the Middle East Could Cost Africa 0.2% in Annual Economic Growth
articles.summary: A joint policy document by four regional organizations the crisis in the Middle East having on the African economies, with growth in the continent forecast to decline by
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly the Israel-Palestine tensions that escalated in October 2023, has disrupted global oil supply chains, impacting African economies reliant on energy imports. Ethiopia, facing its own economic challenges due to internal conflicts and the aftermath of the Tigray War, is particularly vulnerable to these external shocks. The joint policy document by the African Union, ECOWAS, IGAD, and COMESA highlights the interconnectedness of regional stability and economic growth.

articles.title: Zemen Bank Teams up with EIB Global to Mobilise €40mln in Financing for Agribusinesses
articles.summary: Agricultural businesses in Ethiopia will be eligible for as much as €40 million in financing as a result of an agreement between the European Investment Bank Group’s development
articles.context: Ethiopia's agribusiness sector has faced significant challenges due to ongoing conflicts, particularly the Tigray War, which began in November 2020, disrupting supply chains and agricultural production. The recent peace agreement in November 2022 has opened opportunities for economic recovery and investment. The collaboration between Zemen Bank and EIB Global is crucial as it aims to revitalize the agricultural economy, supporting food security and sustainable development in a country grappling with the aftermath of conflict.

articles.title: Korir defends Boston Marathon title with course record
articles.summary: Kenya's John Korir sets a new course record of two hours, one minute and 52 seconds to win a second successive Boston Marathon.
articles.context: John Korir's victory at the Boston Marathon comes amid a resurgence of Kenyan long-distance runners on the global stage, following a challenging period for the sport due to the COVID-19 pandemic. His achievement highlights Kenya's dominance in marathon running, which has been a source of national pride and economic opportunity, especially as the country prepares for the upcoming 2024 Paris Olympics, where athletics will be a key focus.

articles.title: AU Condemns Israeli Diplomatic Envoy to Somaliland
articles.summary: The African Union (AU) and a dozen governments have condemned Israel’s decision to appoint a diplomatic envoy to Somaliland. “The Union does not recognize Somaliland as an independent state,” reads a statement issued by the AU Commission yesterday in response to Israel’s appointment of its first ambassador to Somaliland last week. On April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it remains unrecognized by the international community, including the African Union, which views it as part of Somalia. The recent appointment of an Israeli ambassador to Somaliland marks a significant diplomatic shift and has raised tensions, as it challenges the AU's stance and Somalia's claims over Somaliland. This development comes amidst ongoing efforts by Somaliland to gain international recognition and strengthen its diplomatic ties.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s quiet energy shift: Ethio telecom’s role in building a green future
articles.summary: In the global race toward decarbonization, significant changes are often driven not by traditional energy giants but by agile institutions that blend digital ecosystems with physical infrastructure. In East Africa, a quiet yet substantial energy revolution is taking shape, with Ethio telecom at the forefront. The company is rapidly transforming from a conventional connectivity provider […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been undergoing a significant political transition since the appointment of Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed in 2018, which has included economic reforms and a push for digital transformation. Amid ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and efforts to stabilize the country, Ethio telecom is leveraging its infrastructure to support renewable energy initiatives, aligning with Ethiopia's 2025 Climate Resilient Green Economy Strategy, which aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.

articles.title: EU resumes direct budgetary support to Ethiopia
articles.summary: The European Union has officially resumed its direct budgetary support to Ethiopia, which had been suspended since 2020. This move is regarded as a significant boost to the country’s current reform agenda and marks the beginning of a new chapter in the 50-year strategic partnership between the two entities. The resumption of support will result […]
articles.context: The EU suspended its budgetary support to Ethiopia in 2020 due to concerns over human rights violations amid the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The resumption of aid comes after the signing of a peace agreement in November 2022 between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), signaling a potential stabilization in the region and a renewed commitment to reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration.

articles.title: EU Resumes Budgetary Support to Ethiopian Government
articles.summary: The European Union (EU) has announced it has resumed budget support to Ethiopia, more than five years after the bloc cut payments over concerns of human rights violations during the northern Ethiopia war. The EU is starting the budgetary support with a 140 million Euro allocated for priority sectors. Jozeph Sikela, EU commissioner for international […]
articles.context: The EU's decision to resume budgetary support comes after a prolonged suspension initiated in 2017 due to escalating human rights abuses during the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The Ethiopian government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, signed a peace agreement with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in November 2022, marking a critical step towards stability. This renewed support reflects the EU's recognition of Ethiopia's efforts to rebuild and foster political reconciliation.

articles.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near Kenyan army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
articles.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.
articles.context: The UK has maintained a military presence in Kenya since the 1950s, primarily for training purposes. The 2010 Kenya-UK Security Cooperation Agreement further solidified this relationship, allowing for joint operations against terrorism. However, the legacy of these military activities has led to complex social dynamics, including the acknowledgment of children born to UK soldiers, which has recently gained attention amid ongoing discussions about accountability and the rights of these children.

articles.title: school bus quotes
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, has severely disrupted education and transportation systems. As peace efforts, including the November 2022 Pretoria Agreement, aim to stabilize the region, the safe transport of students remains a pressing concern. Recent incidents involving school buses highlight the challenges faced by families and communities in conflict-affected areas, underscoring the need for reliable and secure educational access amidst ongoing tensions.

articles.title: 'The weapons were loud, but there was always music': Sudanese band play on through the war
articles.summary: One of Sudan's most popular bands, Aswat Almadina, recall being in the studio when the war broke out three years ago.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, the country has descended into chaos. Amidst this turmoil, cultural expressions like music have become vital for resilience and unity, highlighting the stark contrast between the violence of war and the enduring spirit of Sudanese artists like Aswat Almadina.

articles.title: Bondholder, Commercial Creditor Deals Remain Out of Reach in Ethiopia Debt Saga
articles.summary: The co-chairs of Ethiopia’s official creditors committee (OCC) report the country has yet to ink restructuring agreements with bondholders and commercial creditors that comply with its comparability of treatment (CoT) requirements. The committee, chaired by China and France, rejected an agreement in principle reached between the Ethiopian government and holders of its one billion dollar […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a severe debt crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020. The government’s efforts to secure debt restructuring have been complicated by the need to adhere to the G20’s Common Framework for Debt Treatments, which emphasizes comparability of treatment among creditors. The ongoing negotiations with bondholders and commercial creditors are critical as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy and regain access to international financial markets.

articles.title: TPLF Central Committee Convenes in Axum as Administrative Rift Deepens
articles.summary: A high stakes meeting of the proscribed Tigray People Liberation Front (TPLF) Central Committee in Axum is intensifying fears of a political rupture in Tigray, with mounting indications that the party could move to reject the region’s interim administration and push to restore its pre-war government structure. The meeting, which began on April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: The TPLF, once the dominant force in Ethiopia's ruling coalition, has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the federal government since November 2020, leading to widespread humanitarian crises. The November 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities, but tensions remain high as the TPLF's dissatisfaction with the interim administration, established post-conflict, grows. The Axum meeting signals a potential shift towards rejecting the interim governance model and re-establishing TPLF's former authority in Tigray.

articles.title: Education Ministry’s Digital Exam Scheme Triggers Parliamentary Uproar
articles.summary: The Education Ministry has come under intense parliamentary scrutiny over the effectiveness and readiness of its ambitious plan to conduct the upcoming university entrance exam entirely online, set to take place within the next few months. Earlier this year, the Minister of Education announced a landmark shift from paper-based exams to an entirely online modality […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's education sector has faced significant challenges amid ongoing political unrest and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The government's push for digital transformation in education, including the shift to online exams, comes as part of broader reforms aimed at modernizing the system and addressing disparities exacerbated by the conflict. However, concerns about infrastructure and access have intensified scrutiny from parliament, reflecting the fragile state of governance and public trust.

articles.title: Illegal Fuel Trade Booms as Supply Disruptions Leave Drivers Idle
articles.summary: Drivers are paying exorbitant prices for fuel in a parallel market that has only grown more lucrative in the wake of the war in Iran and associated global supply disruptions. An anonymous source working as a driver for a private company told The Reporter that he encountered extreme fuel prices while operating in the Gambella […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly the Tigray War that escalated in November 2020, has severely disrupted supply chains and economic stability. Coupled with the recent war in Iran, which has affected global oil prices, the scarcity of fuel has driven many drivers to rely on a burgeoning parallel market. This situation highlights the vulnerability of Ethiopia's economy and the challenges posed by both local and international conflicts on essential resources.

articles.title: Justice Can’t Wait: Why Dialogue and Accountability Must Go Hand in Hand
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s Ministry of Justice recently confirmed that the country’s transitional justice process will begin only after the conclusion of the ongoing national dialogue, which is scheduled to conclude in October 2026. At first glance, this sequencing may appear logical: dialogue first, justice later. Yet, this approach potentially undermines both processes. Transitional justice and national dialogue […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, since the outbreak of the civil war in November 2020, which has resulted in widespread human rights abuses. The government initiated a peace agreement with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in November 2022, but ongoing ethnic tensions and violence continue to threaten stability. The transitional justice process is crucial for addressing these grievances, yet its delay until after the national dialogue raises concerns about accountability.

articles.title: No African Consensus on Transboundary Waters, Security: Ethiopia
articles.summary: AU requests AUDA-NEPAD for technical, financial support for Ethiopia’s COP32 preparations Ethiopia has raised its concerns regarding lack of African consensus on the nexus of climate change and security, particularly pertaining to transboundary waters and security. The AU has disclosed documents compiling the decisions, declarations and resolutions made during the 39th Assembly of the Union […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's concerns about the lack of African consensus on transboundary waters stem from ongoing tensions over the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD), which has been a point of contention with downstream countries Sudan and Egypt since its construction began in 2011. The AU's involvement, particularly through the 39th Assembly of the Union held in February 2022, highlights the urgent need for collaborative frameworks addressing climate change impacts on regional security and water resources.

articles.title: UN relief chief warns South Sudan nearing famine as crisis deepens
articles.summary: 'The world’s youngest nation stands at a dangerous crossroads,' Tom Fletcher tells Security Council
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to widespread displacement and humanitarian crises. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to stabilize the country, but implementation has faltered, with violence persisting and economic conditions deteriorating. As of late 2023, the combination of political instability and climate-related shocks has pushed millions towards the brink of famine, prompting urgent warnings from UN officials.

articles.title: UN official warns of rising violence, mounting humanitarian crisis in South Sudan
articles.summary: 'Security situation remains deeply concerning ...Civilians continue to bear the brunt,' says head of UN Mission in South Sudan
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013 between forces loyal to President Salva Kiir and those aligned with former Vice President Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to stabilize the country, but violence has surged again, particularly in regions like Jonglei and Unity, leading to a humanitarian crisis affecting millions, with over 7 million people in need of assistance.

articles.title: OIC condemns Israeli move to appoint envoy in Somaliland
articles.summary: Organization of Islamic Cooperation denounces Israel’s reported plan to appoint envoy in Somaliland, calling it a violation of Somalia’s sovereignty
articles.context: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has historically supported Somalia's territorial integrity, especially following Somaliland's unilateral declaration of independence in 1991, which has not been recognized internationally. The recent announcement of Israel's intention to appoint an envoy in Somaliland, a region seeking international recognition, threatens to exacerbate tensions between the Somali federal government and Somaliland authorities, complicating ongoing reconciliation efforts and regional dynamics involving neighboring countries like Ethiopia and Djibouti.

articles.title: Somalia says Israeli move on Somaliland violates sovereignty, risks regional stability
articles.summary: State Minister Ali Mohammad Omar says Mogadishu rejects recognition of breakaway region, warns of security risks in Red Sea corridor
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it has not received international recognition, leading to ongoing tensions between Mogadishu and Hargeisa. The recent announcement by Israel to strengthen ties with Somaliland, including potential diplomatic recognition, has heightened concerns in Somalia about the implications for regional stability, particularly in light of the 2020 normalization agreements between Israel and several Arab states. Somalia's government fears this move could embolden separatist sentiments and disrupt security in the strategically vital Red Sea corridor.

articles.title: Somalia condemns Israeli ambassador's appointment to its breakaway region of Somaliland
articles.summary: Mogadishu declares Tel Aviv's move violation of Somalia’s sovereignty, unity, and territorial integrity, urging international bodies to uphold international law, as it clearly contradicts UN Charter, African Union's foundational principles
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it has not received international recognition, leading to ongoing tensions between Mogadishu and Hargeisa. The recent appointment of an Israeli ambassador to Somaliland is seen as a direct challenge to Somalia's sovereignty, especially following the 2020 normalization agreements between Israel and several Arab states, which have shifted regional alliances. Somalia's government, led by President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud since May 2022, emphasizes the need for international support to uphold its territorial integrity.

articles.title: Airstrikes in Somalia kill 54 al-Shabaab terrorists
articles.summary: Operations also targeted supply stockpiles, significantly weakening group’s logistical capacity, mobility, ability to organize attacks, says Defense Ministry
articles.context: The recent airstrikes come amid intensified efforts by the Somali government and African Union forces to combat al-Shabaab, particularly following the 2022 agreement between Somalia and the African Union to bolster military cooperation. The offensive is part of a broader campaign launched in 2021 under President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud, aimed at reclaiming territory and dismantling the group’s operational capabilities, which have been a persistent threat to stability in the region.

articles.title: Israel appoints 1st ambassador to Somalia's breakaway region in controversial move
articles.summary: Move comes after Tel Aviv announced decision to recognize Somaliland in December 2025
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991 but has not been internationally recognized, leading to ongoing tensions between the two. In December 2025, Israel's recognition of Somaliland marked a significant shift in diplomatic relations, as it aligns with Somaliland's aspirations for statehood and could influence other nations' stances. This move is particularly controversial given Somalia's longstanding claim over Somaliland and the fragile political dynamics in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: UN chief calls Sudan conflict world’s ‘largest humanitarian crisis’ 3 years on
articles.summary: ‘Nearly 34 million people inside Sudan now need humanitarian assistance,’ Antonio Guterres says
articles.context: The conflict in Sudan escalated dramatically in April 2023 when fighting broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and displacement. This turmoil follows years of political instability after the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019 and the subsequent struggle for power among military factions. The ongoing crisis has severely hindered humanitarian efforts, exacerbating the needs of millions already facing food insecurity and health challenges.

articles.title: 5 miners killed in gold mine collapse in eastern Sudan, medics say
articles.summary: More than 25 people remain trapped in Red Sea state amid limited rescue resources
articles.context: The collapse of the gold mine in eastern Sudan highlights the ongoing challenges in the region, exacerbated by the 2021 military coup that destabilized the transitional government. The Red Sea state has seen increased informal mining activities as economic conditions worsen, with groups like the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces vying for control over lucrative resources. This incident underscores the dire need for improved safety regulations and humanitarian support in conflict-affected areas.

articles.title: 'Don’t forget Sudan': African Union chair presses for ceasefire push
articles.summary: 'Sudan is passing through disastrous situation,' Mahmoud Ali Youssouf tells Anadolu, adding that lack of media coverage and shifting attention to Mideast conflict is leading to concerns
articles.context: Sudan has been embroiled in conflict since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in April 2019, leading to a power struggle between military and civilian factions. The recent outbreak of violence in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces has exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, displacing millions. The African Union's call for a ceasefire comes amid concerns that the international community's focus is shifting to the Israel-Palestine conflict, sidelining Sudan's urgent needs.

articles.title: Sudan slams Germany-hosted conference as ‘colonial tutelage approach’
articles.summary: Foreign Ministry says hosting conference without consulting Khartoum reflects ‘disregard’ for Sudan
articles.context: Sudan has been grappling with political instability since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in April 2019, leading to a power struggle between military and civilian factions. The ongoing conflict in Darfur and the recent coup in October 2021 have further complicated the situation. The German-hosted conference, aimed at addressing Sudan's transition and international support, has drawn criticism from Khartoum, which perceives it as undermining its sovereignty and disregarding its role in the peace process.

articles.title: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
articles.summary: As the conflict enters its fourth year, journalist Mohamed Suleiman shudders at what has been lost.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, the country has descended into chaos. The war has resulted in widespread displacement, humanitarian crises, and a breakdown of communication, making the messages from trapped journalist Mohamed Suleiman particularly poignant as they reflect the dire realities faced by civilians.

articles.title: The human cost of the war in Sudan, three years on
articles.summary: The conflict, which erupted in 2023, has left behind a human toll which is "simply staggering", reports the BBC's Barbara Plett Usher.
articles.context: The war in Sudan began in April 2023, primarily between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo. This conflict erupted amid a fragile transition to democracy following the ousting of longtime dictator Omar al-Bashir in 2019, exacerbating existing ethnic tensions and humanitarian crises. The ongoing violence has displaced millions and disrupted aid efforts, drawing international concern and intervention attempts.

articles.title: FACTBOX - 3 years of war: The human cost in Sudan
articles.summary: Sudan’s civil war has uprooted nearly 15M people and killed over an estimated 150,000 as the conflict enters its 4th year with over 33M in need of humanitarian aid and 21M facing a deepening food crisis
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in April 2021 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, the struggle for power has exacerbated existing ethnic tensions and economic instability. The 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir, following months of protests, set the stage for a fragile transition that ultimately collapsed, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The ongoing clashes have hindered peace efforts and complicated regional relations, particularly with neighboring South Sudan and Egypt.

articles.title: Fire at Sudan’s Darfur displacement camp leaves 350 families homeless
articles.summary: Blaze in South Darfur camp destroys shelters and forces hundreds of families to relocate amid worsening humanitarian conditions
articles.context: The fire at the displacement camp in South Darfur comes amid escalating violence in the region, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This ongoing conflict has exacerbated humanitarian crises, displacing millions and straining resources. The situation is further complicated by the 2020 Juba Peace Agreement, which has failed to bring lasting stability, leaving vulnerable populations like those in Darfur in dire need of assistance.

articles.title: Africa harder hit by Iran war than Germany, says Chancellor Merz
articles.summary: Africa faces looming inflation and food crisis, warns German leader ahead of conference on civil war in Sudan
articles.context: The ongoing civil war in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and displacement. This conflict has disrupted agricultural production, exacerbating food insecurity across Africa. As the war continues, the ripple effects threaten to drive inflation and food crises in neighboring countries, prompting urgent discussions at the upcoming international conference on Sudan's humanitarian situation.

articles.title: Somali forces kill 27 al-Shabaab terrorists in 'large-scale operation': Defense Ministry
articles.summary: Key members of terrorist group killed, weapons, military equipment seized
articles.context: The operation comes amid intensified efforts by the Somali government, supported by African Union forces, to combat al-Shabaab, which has been waging an insurgency since 2006. The recent offensive aligns with the Somali National Army's "Operation Dhuulow," launched in August 2023, aimed at reclaiming territory and dismantling the group's influence ahead of the 2024 elections. This military action is crucial for stabilizing the region and restoring public confidence in the government.

articles.title: Red Cross warns of worsening Sudan crisis as displacement shifts
articles.summary: ‘The needs are overwhelming. Sudan cannot be forgotten’ says secretary-general of Sudanese Red Crescent
articles.context: Sudan has been engulfed in conflict since April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. Over 5 million people have been displaced, exacerbating an already dire situation stemming from years of political instability and economic hardship. The recent ceasefire agreements, including the Jeddah Declaration in May 2023, have failed to bring lasting peace, leaving millions in urgent need of assistance.

articles.title: الذكرى الثالثة للحرب في السودان: رسائل لن تصل
articles.summary: تشير تقديرات ميدانية وتحليلية شاملة لمشروع "ACLED" والتحقيقات الصحفية الدولية (مثل نيويورك تايمز) المنشورة في مطلع عام 2026 إلى أن الرقم الفعلي للضحايا تجاوز 150,000 قتيل عند احتساب الوفيات الناتجة عن الجوع والمرض وانهيار النظام الصحي.
articles.context: تحتفل السودان في أبريل 2026 بالذكرى الثالثة لاندلاع الحرب الأهلية التي بدأت في أبريل 2023، بعد صراع طويل بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. تصاعدت التوترات بعد انهيار اتفاقية السلام في 2021، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. يشير تقرير "ACLED" إلى أن النزاع أدى إلى وفاة أكثر من 150,000 شخص، مما يعكس الكارثة الإنسانية المستمرة في البلاد.

articles.title: Wildberries Opens its E-commerce Platform to Sellers in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Eurasia’s digital marketplace Wildberries has expanded operations into Ethiopia, with the opening of its e-commerce platform to sellers in the East African country. The Wildberries marketplace will feature
articles.context: Wildberries' entry into Ethiopia comes amid a critical period of economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. This expansion aligns with Ethiopia's broader efforts to enhance digital commerce and attract foreign investment, particularly as the government seeks to stabilize the economy and create jobs. The move also reflects increasing interest from international companies in the Horn of Africa's emerging markets.

articles.title: Wegagen Bank Partners IFC to Boost its Trade Financing
articles.summary: Wegagen Bank has signed a strategic partnership agreement with the International Finance Corporation (IFC), a member of the World Bank Group, for the provision of trade finance guarantee
articles.context: Wegagen Bank's partnership with the IFC comes at a crucial time as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy following the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022. The agreement, signed in October 2023, aims to enhance trade financing amid ongoing economic challenges, including inflation and foreign exchange shortages. Strengthening financial institutions like Wegagen Bank is vital for Ethiopia's recovery and growth in a post-conflict environment.

articles.title: Afreximbank to Support Dangote’s Vision as the Group Eyes $100 Billion Revenue by 2030
articles.summary: African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank) announced that it is supporting Dangote Group, as it seeks to expand its operations and grow its turnover to US$100 billion by 2030. The
articles.context: Afreximbank's support for Dangote Group comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022. The Ethiopian government is focusing on revitalizing its economy through foreign investments and partnerships, particularly in the manufacturing sector. Dangote's ambitious revenue target aligns with the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement, which aims to boost intra-African trade and economic integration, making this collaboration particularly timely.

articles.title: EEU Plans building Massive Electric Vehicle charging network
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – The Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU), a state-owned electricity service provider, is set to build a massive electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure network as part of
articles.context: Ethiopia's push for an electric vehicle charging network comes amid a broader national strategy to enhance sustainable energy usage, following the 2021 signing of the Climate Change Strategy. This initiative aligns with the government's commitment to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 64% by 2030, particularly in the wake of ongoing regional tensions and the need for economic recovery post-conflict in Tigray. articles.title: «التعليم العالي» في السودان تنفي استيلاءها على أموال الجامعات
articles.summary: الخرطوم 18 مارس 2026 – نفت وزارة التعليم العالي والبحث العلمي في السودان، الأربعاء، مزاعم استيلائها على جزء من الدعم الإضافي الذي خصصته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وتحدث ناشطون في مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي عن استيلاء الوزارة على 40% من الدعم المؤسسي الذي قدمته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وخصصت وزارة المالية دعماً إضافياً للجامعات في إطار موازنة العام الحالي، لتحسين الوضع المالي لأعضاء هيئة التدريس والكوادر المساعدة وتأهيلهم وتدريبهم، على أن يُصرف حسب المعايير التي توصي بها لجنة التمويل ويجيزها المجلس القومي للتعليم العالي والبحث العلمي. وقالت وزارة التعليم العالي، في بيان، إن “الدعم الإضافي ما زال محفوظاً ولن يتم التصرف فيه لحين إكمال قاعدة بيانات...

articles.title: تشاد تتوعد بالرد بعد هجوم لمسيّرة سودانية على «الطينة»
articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: «أدري».. من شريان حياة إلى بوابة مؤصدة: جحيم الحصار يطارد نازحي دارفور
articles.summary: أدري 18 مارس 2026- أصدرت الحكومة التشادية في فبراير الماضي قراراً فاجأ آلاف النازحين السودانيين العالقين على حدودها الشرقية، بإعلانها إغلاق جميع المعابر الحدودية مع السودان “حتى إشعار آخر”. وكان منفذ “أدري”، الذي يعد الشريان الرئيسي الذي يعبره الفارون من جحيم الحرب في دارفور، أول المغلَقين. لم يكن القرار تشادياً خالصاً، بل جاء تتويجاً لأسابيع من التوتر المتصاعد على الحدود، أدت إلى سقوط قتلى في صفوف الجنود التشاديين. وبين ليلة وضحاها، تحول معبر الأمل إلى بوابة مؤصدة، تاركاً مئات العائلات عالقة في العراء، بين جحيم الحرب خلفها وبوابة مغلقة أمامها.

articles.title: أطفال السودان في رمضان.. طفولة سرقتها الحرب
articles.summary: منتدى الإعلام السوداني بورتسودان، مدني، 18 مارس 2026 (سودان تربيون)– لم تكن آمنة محمد تتخيل أن يأتي رمضان يومًا وهي تحاول إقناع أطفالها بأن القليل من الطعام يكفي للإفطار. نزحت آمنة مع أطفالها الثلاثة من منزلهم في الخرطوم قبل ثلاث سنوات، بعد اشتداد القتال بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، لتجد نفسها في أحد مراكز الإيواء بمدينة بورتسودان، حيث تغيرت ملامح الشهر الكريم تمامًا بالنسبة لأطفالها. تقول آمنة بصوت يختلط فيه الحزن بالصبر: “كان أطفالي ينتظرون رمضان كل عام لشراء الفوانيس والملابس الجديدة، لكن هذا العام لا نفكر إلا في توفير الطعام. أحيانًا لا نجد ما يكفي للإفطار.

articles.title: ايصال أدوية لدعم (80) ألف طفل في «طويلة» غرب السودان
articles.summary: طويلة 18 مارس 2026 – أعلنت منظمة أنقذوا الأطفال، الأربعاء، عن إيصال أدوية لدعم 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ لمدة 6 أشهر في طويلة بولاية شمال دارفور غربي السودان. وتحوّلت طويلة، الخاضعة لسيطرة حركة تحرير السودان بقيادة عبد الواحد نور، من بلدات نائية تفتقر إلى الخدمات إلى مأوى لأكثر من 715 ألف نازح، معظمهم فرّوا من الهجمات على الفاشر ومخيمات النزوح المجاورة لها. وقالت المنظمة، في بيان، إنها أوصلت “شحنة من بورتسودان إلى طويلة لدعم أكثر من 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ في طويلة، حيث إنها كافية للإبقاء على عمل 20 مرفقًا صحيًا وعيادات متنقلة تخدم المجتمعات النازحة لمدة ستة أشهر”. وأشارت إلى أن الشحنة، التي تحمل 30 طنًا من الأدوية وإمدادات التغذية، وصلت...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.summary: النهود 17 مارس 2026- أقدمت قوات الدعم السريع، الثلاثاء، على إحراق قافلة تجارية تضم شاحنات كبيرة محملة بمحاصيل زراعية بالقرب من مدينة النهود بولاية غرب كردفان، أثناء توجهها إلى الأبيض بولاية شمال كردفان. ووثق عناصر من القوات عبر مقطع فيديو لحظة إضرام النار في الشاحنات التي كانت محملة بكميات كبيرة من المحاصيل الزراعية، بما في ذلك الذرة والفول السوداني. وتُعد النهود وغبيش وودبندة بولاية غرب كردفان من أبرز مناطق إنتاج الفول في السودان، كما تضم النهود واحدة من أكبر بورصات تداول المحاصيل في غرب السودان، غير أنها تعرضت لعمليات نهب واسعة عقب سيطرة الدعم السريع على المنطقة، الأمر الذي أدى إلى اضطراب النشاط التجاري ودفع العديد من التجار إلى مغادرة...

articles.title: السودان : معدل التضخم يواصل التراجع
articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.
